summary: I explored the repo to understand the dark mode toggle's current implementation and placement. The toggle is a single shared partial (`_theme_toggle.html.erb`) backed by a Stimulus controller, a cookie-based `theme_helper`, and a pre-paint init script — no duplication issues there. It's currently rendered in two places: right-aligned in the sidebar's top bar (main app layout) and inline next to the heading in the auth/sign-in layout. The key finding is that "top right corner" is ambiguous given the app's structure: there's no persistent app-wide header spanning the viewport, and the style guide already reserves each page's own top-right slot for a "one primary action per page." I wrote discovery notes to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/code-explorer/output/discovery_notes.md` covering the current implementation, both call sites, the structural ambiguity, three open questions about what "top right corner" should mean (sidebar-relative, viewport-relative, or per-page-header), and constraints from the UI style guide and existing system test.
